artifact.schema_coverage: none; EffectiveRecord objects are outside source model 0.2.0 and the current JSON Schema/validator
artifact.review_note: Module 3.4 independent review completed in REV-009. All four EffectiveRecords are accepted with review_status=reviewed.

effective_records.effective_text_en: If two pharmacologically relevant species exist for the clinical candidate, one rodent and one non-rodent, both species should be used for short-term general toxicology studies of up to 1 month.
effective_records.synthesis_rationale: This EffectiveRecord represents one operative proposition from the reviewed narrows mapping ich_s6_r1.amend.002. Parent record ich_s6_r1.kr.part1.3_3.006 supplies the general expectation that safety evaluation programs should normally include two relevant species. Addendum record ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.2_2.001 narrows the effective statement to the condition in ich_s6_r1.cond.part2.2_2.001 and the duration criterion in ich_s6_r1.qc.part2.2_2.004. The effective text preserves the Addendum source modality 'should be used' and does not convert it to a descriptive phrase.

effective_records.effective_text_en: When no relevant species exists, relevant transgenic animals expressing the human receptor or homologous proteins should be considered. Absence of interaction with the orthologous target in any species is one situation in which no relevant species can be identified.
effective_records.synthesis_rationale: This EffectiveRecord represents one operative proposition from the reviewed clarifies mapping ich_s6_r1.amend.003. The Parent 'should be considered' recommendation remains operative. The Addendum's 'can be considered' wording is treated as a clarifying restatement of the same pathway and supplies the orthologous-target precondition; it is not treated as a superseding reduction in normative force. The synthesis includes both the Parent no-relevant-species precondition and the Addendum orthologous-target precondition.
representation_limitations: Cross-reference ich_s6_r1.xref.part2.2_1.002 refers to the ICH S6 Guideline and remains unresolved at target_id level in the source model; it is reviewed as an unresolved guideline reference, not a substantive uncertainty in this EffectiveRecord.

edition_context.document_version_label: Current Step 4 version; Parent Guideline dated 16 July 1997; Addendum dated 12 June 2011 incorporated at the end of June 2011

effective_records.effective_text_en: For monoclonal-antibody testing, expression of the desired epitope remains central to species relevance. An epitope-negative species may still have some relevance for toxicity assessment if comparable unintended tissue cross-reactivity to humans is demonstrated. However, assessment of tissue cross-reactivity in animal tissues has limited value for species selection and TCR studies can be used only in specific cases where other approaches cannot demonstrate a pharmacologically relevant species. When unexpected binding occurs in human tissues, selected animal tissues can provide supplemental information, but TCR using a full panel of animal tissues is not recommended.
effective_records.synthesis_rationale: This EffectiveRecord represents one operative proposition from the reviewed narrows mapping ich_s6_r1.amend.004. Parent records ich_s6_r1.kr.part1.3_3.004 and ich_s6_r1.kr.part1.3_3.005 supply the monoclonal-antibody epitope-expression criterion and the conditional relevance of an epitope-negative species when comparable unintended tissue cross-reactivity to humans is demonstrated. Parent Conditions ich_s6_r1.cond.part1.3_3.002 and ich_s6_r1.cond.part1.3_3.003 remain material to that scope. Addendum records ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.2_1.007 and ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.2_1.008 narrow the role of animal-tissue TCR to limited species-selection value and specific cases where other approaches cannot demonstrate a pharmacologically relevant species. Note 1 records ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.notes.005 and ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.notes.014 limit full-panel animal TCR and permit selected-animal-tissue evaluation only as conditional supplemental information when unexpected binding occurs in human tissues. The effective text preserves 'can be used' and does not normalize it to 'may'.
representation_limitations: Cross-reference ich_s6_r1.xref.part2.2_1.001 has raw text '(see Note 1)' and target_id=null because model 0.2.0 cannot resolve note-level targets. The linked Note 1 source unit ich_s6_r1.su.part2.notes.note1.004 and operative Note 1 records are included, so this is a representation limitation rather than absence of reviewed context.

effective_records.effective_text_en: If two species have been used to assess the safety of an ADC, an additional short-term study or arm should be conducted in at least one species with the unconjugated toxin. A rodent is preferred unless the toxin is not active in the rodent.
effective_records.synthesis_rationale: This EffectiveRecord is Addendum-only guidance; no meaningful Parent counterpart exists, so amendment_relation_ids is empty. Record ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.notes.009 supplies the short-term study or arm recommendation, Condition ich_s6_r1.cond.part2.notes.005 supplies the two-species precondition, and QuantitativeCriterion ich_s6_r1.qc.part2.notes.001 supplies the at-least-one-species criterion for the unconjugated toxin. Record ich_s6_r1.kr.part2.notes.015 supplies the rodent preference, and Condition ich_s6_r1.cond.part2.notes.006 supplies the exception when the toxin is not active in the rodent. The novel-toxin general-principles statement is a separate proposition and is excluded from this EffectiveRecord.
